title: Stockdale Paradox

content.statement: Hold unwavering faith you will prevail while confronting the most brutal facts of your reality.
content.question: What brutal facts are you avoiding, and does your team know you still believe you will prevail?
content.equation: Resilience = Brutal Honesty × Long-term Faith × Daily Action
content.mental_model: Two hands, one holds hope, the other holds reality.
content.business_lens: In hard quarters, name the brutal facts publicly inside the company while reaffirming the long-term mission.
content.life_lens: Confront what is true today without losing the belief that you will get through it.
sop: Gather honest data without softening it
Communicate the brutal facts to the team
Make a 90-day plan addressing each fact
Track and adjust without losing faith
content.explainer: Stockdale survived years of imprisonment because he refused both false optimism (those who believed they would be home by Christmas died of broken hearts) and despair. Companies that confront brutal facts while maintaining unshakeable mission belief outperform during downturns.
questions: What brutal facts are you avoiding, and does your team know you still believe you will prevail?
What number are you currently rounding up or softening when you report it to your team?
If your best year and worst year both happened this quarter, which one are you actually prepared for?

definitions.def: The discipline of holding unwavering faith that you will prevail in the end while simultaneously confronting the most brutal facts of your current reality.

content.equation_example: Rate each 1-10. Brutal Honesty 8, Long-term Faith 7, Daily Action 6. Score = 8 times 7 times 6 = 336 out of a possible 1,000. Higher means the team can absorb hard news without losing motivation. Lower means either false optimism or quiet despair is running the room.
content.closing_statement: Face the facts without losing the faith.

caption:
Principle Nº 275: Stockdale Paradox

Hold unwavering faith you will prevail while confronting the most brutal facts of your reality. Stockdale survived years of imprisonment because he refused both false optimism (those who believed they would be home by Christmas died of broken hearts) and despair.

Ask yourself: What brutal facts are you avoiding, and does your team know you still believe you will prevail?
